rationale: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B is a hybrid MoE model: 40 layers, of which 10 are full_attention (every 4th, idx 3/7/11/15/19/23/27/31/35/39) and 30 are linear_attention (GatedDeltaNet). RedKnot full-attention head-class sparsity is applied ONLY to the full layers. Shallow full layers carry critical early features, so we keep the FIRST HALF+1 full layers DENSE (exact) and sparsify only the DEEP half. Verified: keeping the first 6 of 10 full layers dense + head-class (frac_global=0.4, window=4096) on the deep 4 is lossless on the 35B (dF1=0 full-only; combined with linear windowing dF1~-0.10).
notes: dense_full_layers = ceil(num_full_layers/2)+1 = 6. Sparse layers use frac_global=0.4 (40% kv-heads global / full attention) + window=4096 (60% kv-heads local sliding window). Linear-attention sparsity is configured separately in sparse_ffn_params/qwen3.5-35B-A3B.json (per-head token window with decayed-prefix reuse).

sweet_spot.selection: safety=2.0, deep_start=8, mass_thresh=0.7 preserved F1 on selected triviaqa/multifieldqa_en scans and raised total FLOPs save to 38-45%; more aggressive than the prior default, but hotpotqa@32K is excluded because this setting dropped that sample.
